zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir David Graham - Laird Of Dundaff於1210年之前出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾時，他的父親David Graham24歲，母親Agnes Nobles19歲。他於1240年與Annabelle Sinclair在蘇格蘭費伏結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1270年4月27日在蘇格蘭培爾金加迪，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.Dundaff Castle, Dunure, Ayrshire, Scotla: 英国苏格兰艾斯爾登努
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir David Graham - Laird Of Dundaff于1210年之前出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾时，他的父亲David Graham24岁，母亲Agnes Nobles19岁。他于1240年与Annabelle Sinclair在苏格兰費伏结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1270年4月27日在苏格兰培爾金加迪，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.
Scottish: variant of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Laird
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: status name for a landlord or landowner, from northern Middle English laverd ‘lord’.

variants.name: Lard
meanings: Scottish: altered form of   Laird  .

variants.name: Leard
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Laird  .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir David Graham - Laird Of Dundaff was born before 1210, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, David Graham, was 24 and his mother, Agnes Nobles, was 19. He married Annabelle Sinclair in 1240, in Fife, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 27 April 1270, in Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir David Graham - Laird Of Dundaff est né avant 1210, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, David Graham, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Agnes Nobles, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Annabelle Sinclair en 1240, à Fife, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 27 avril 1270, à Kincardine, Perthshire, Écosse, et a été enterré à Écosse.

Er hat 1240 in Fife, Scotland Annabelle Sinclair geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 27. April 1270 in Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

Lui sposato Annabelle Sinclair nel 1240, nel Fife, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 27 aprile 1270, nel Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir David Graham - Laird Of Dundaffが1210年以前にイギリススコットランドエイルシュアイアで生まれた時，父David Grahamは24歳，母Agnes Noblesは19歳であった。1240年にスコットランドフィフでAnnabelle Sinclairと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1270年4月27日にスコットランドペルスシュアイアキンカーディンで死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir David Graham - Laird Of Dundaff(이)가 1210년 이전 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David Graham(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Nobles(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1240년 스컷랜드 피프에서 Annabelle Sinclair(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1270년 4월 27일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 킨카딘에서 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Annabelle Sinclair em 1240, em Fife, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir David Graham - Laird Of Dundaff родился перед 1210 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени David Graham было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Nobles, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Annabelle Sinclair в 1240 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 27 апреля 1270 г. в местности кинкардаин, пиртшир, Шотландия и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.
